perturbation.original_text: 2. Non-competition (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective customers in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Non-competition (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective clients in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'customers' to 'clients' in subsection (iii) of the non-competition clause introduces ambiguity. Throughout the rest of the section, 'customers' is used. This change suggests that the restriction might not apply to sales to former clients, potentially narrowing the scope of the non-competition agreement unintentionally. This contradiction creates confusion on whether the company can be doing business with former clients, or just customers. The 'customers' vs 'clients' within section 2(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Non-competition (a) Without the prior written consent of CMC (which may be withheld in CMC's sole discretion), for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, neither Valco nor Brubaker shall, directly or indirectly, whether as a stockholder, individual, partner, agent, representative, employee, employer, director, officer, principal, consultant, advisor, or independent contractor, or through any of the foregoing, or in any other relation or capacity whatsoever: (I) engage in the business relating to sand and gravel mining or sales of ready mix concrete, asphalt and construction aggregates, in Pueblo and/or El Paso Counties, Colorado; (ii) operate or own a concrete batch plant, aggregates operation or asphalt plant in Teller County, Colorado; or (iii) except to Valco's present customers and prospective clients in similar businesses, make any sales to any customers in Teller County, Colorado.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'customers' to 'clients' in Section 2(a)(iii) creates a potential contradiction because the term 'customers' is used consistently throughout the rest of Section 2(a). This inconsistency introduces ambiguity regarding whether the non-competition agreement applies to former clients or only current customers. This directly aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' as the interchangeable use of 'customers' and 'clients' without clear definition leads to conflicting interpretations and thus a strong contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 3. Non-disclosure (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is confidential (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Non-disclosure (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is private (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'confidential' to 'private' introduces a subtle contradiction. The section then defines all such information as "Confidential Information." By using 'private' and then defining it as "Confidential Information", it's now contradictory. It creates an in-text contradiction of what they are reffering to. This contradiction resides within section 3(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Non-disclosure (a) Except as provided in Subsection (b) below, each of Valco and Brubaker agrees that, for a period of ten (10) years from and after the date hereof, all information previously or hereafter disclosed to any of them by CMC in connection with the transactions contemplated by the Acquisition Agreement and Lease and information relating to Valco's (after the date hereof CMC's) Pueblo operations is private (collectively, "Confidential Information") and shall be held in strict confidence and not disclosed to any person or entity.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'confidential' with 'private' in Section 3(a), but then immediately defines the 'private' information as "Confidential Information." This creates an in-text contradiction because the text uses a different adjective to describe the information but still defines it under the original term, which is a clear misalignment of terminology and causes an in-text contradiction as defined by the instructions. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, but this is acceptable as the perturbation explanation explains how the contradiction resides within section 3(a).

perturbation.original_text: 4. Enforcement; Damages; Construction (a) Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco. If Brubaker no longer controls Valco, Valco's and Brubaker's obligation herein shall be several. Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this Agreement. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof. Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in connection with the enforcement of the provisions hereof relating to a breach of this Agreement by Valco or Brubaker.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Enforcement; Damages; Construction (a) Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco. If Brubaker no longer controls Valco, Valco's and Brubaker's obligation herein shall be several. Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this contract. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof. Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in connection with the enforcement of the provisions hereof relating to a breach of this Agreement by Valco or Brubaker.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'Agreement' to 'contract' in the first sentence relating to damages, and leaving it as 'Agreement' in the last sentence, it becomes contradictory. This creates ambiguity about which document dictates the rules for breaches, and whether both terms apply or if the agreement is being split into different documents. The contradiction resides within section 4(a).
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4. Enforcement; Damages; Construction (a) Valco's and Brubaker's obligations hereunder shall be joint and several as long as Brubaker controls Valco. If Brubaker no longer controls Valco, Valco's and Brubaker's obligation herein shall be several. Each of Valco and Brubaker recognizes that it would be impossible to measure in money all the damages which will accrue to CMC by reason of a failure to comply with the restrictions and perform the obligations under this contract. Each of Valco and Brubaker hereby acknowledges that CMC would lack an adequate remedy at law and CMC shall, in addition to and not in lieu of money damages, be entitled to specific performance and injunctive relief against Valco and Brubaker in an action or procedure to enforce the provisions hereof. Valco and Brubaker shall reimburse CMC for its expenses, including reasonable attorney's fees, incurred in connection with the enforcement of the provisions hereof relating to a breach of this Agreement by Valco or Brubaker.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'contract' in the first sentence regarding damages, while keeping it as 'Agreement' in the last sentence within the same section, introduces ambiguity. This creates a contradiction within section 4(a) as it's unclear whether breaches are governed by the 'Agreement', the 'contract', or both. This aligns with the Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction definition, as the interchangeable use of 'Agreement' and 'contract' without clear definition leads to conflicting interpretations of obligations, particularly regarding breaches and enforcement, within the same section (4(a)).
